hook: Your AI gives everyone the same answer. Including you.
caption:
Your AI gives everyone the same answer. Including you.

Ask ChatGPT to help you write an email and it writes... an email. Technically correct. Completely not you.

Too formal. Too long. Uses words like 'circling back' when you'd literally never say that out loud.

So you spend 10 minutes editing it down to what you actually wanted to say in the first place.

This isn't an AI problem. It's a calibration problem.

AI defaults to the average of everyone who's ever asked a similar question. And 'average' is no one's actual voice.

The people getting genuinely useful AI responses aren't prompting harder. They're not writing 400-word instructions every single time.

They've told the AI who they are once — and that context travels with every single ask.

That's the whole idea behind BootFile. You think a certain way. You communicate a certain way. Your AI should already know that before you type a single word.

Not because you trained a model. Because you gave it a profile that actually reflects how your brain works.

One setup. Every response feels like it gets you.
